topics.title: Media Reports and Satellite Radio Rumors
topics.summary: The studio discusses recent media reports regarding Howard Stern's career, specifically addressing rumors of a return to terrestrial radio and the accuracy of data cited by the New York Post.

topics.title: Howard Stern Satellite Radio Rumors
topics.summary: The studio discusses reports regarding Howard Stern potentially returning to terrestrial radio and his subsequent denial of the rumors while addressing media coverage from outlets like Fox News.

topics.title: Jim McGreevy and Satellite Radio Growth
topics.summary: The studio reviews clips of Jim McGreevy's public coming out, discusses the growth of satellite radio over terrestrial broadcasting, and analyzes television ratings for shows like Deal or No Deal.

topics.title: Radio Show Discussion and Personal Reflections
topics.summary: The studio features a conversation about television shows, satellite radio statistics, upcoming racing events, and personal reflections on physical appearance.

topics.title: Host and Staff Discuss Show News
topics.summary: The studio discusses listener feedback regarding the staff, a segment on Sirius radio availability, and various reports from the newsroom.

topics.title: Italian Elvis Reunion and Radio Industry News
topics.summary: The studio features a phone call with an Italian Elvis impersonator who recounts a past performance at Madison Square Garden, followed by a discussion regarding radio ownership and upcoming guests.

topics.title: Television Ratings and Jackass Promotion
topics.summary: The studio discusses the television ratings of Oprah Winfrey versus Ellen DeGeneres, the decline of physical CD sales, and features an interview with Johnny Knoxville regarding his role in the Jackass franchise.

topics.title: Jackass 2 Production and Stunts
topics.summary: The studio discusses the financial structure of the Jackass franchise, the high costs of stunt insurance, and Johnny Knoxville’s experiences with dangerous stunts including a snake bite and a rocket explosion.

topics.title: Johnny Knoxville and Jackass Promotion
topics.summary: The studio features Johnny Knoxville discussing his experiences on the Jackass franchise, including physical risks, interactions with other cast members like Steve-O and Bam, and personal anecdotes regarding substance use and celebrity friendships.

topics.title: Jackass Film Promotion and Sports Commentary
topics.summary: The studio features an interview with Johnny Knoxville regarding the Jackass movie, followed by a discussion on sports broadcasting and weight loss.

topics.title: Sports Broadcasting and Staff Appearance Rankings
topics.summary: The studio features a discussion on the perceived competence of female sports broadcasters followed by a lighthearted ranking of the show's staff members based on their physical appearance.

topics.title: Radio Show Prank Calls and Ranking Lists
topics.summary: The studio features various prank call recordings involving a fake cab company and a TV preacher, followed by a debate over the ranking of male and female colleagues based on physical appearance.

topics.title: Steve Irwin Memorial Tributes
topics.summary: The studio reviews various celebrity tributes and messages honoring the legacy of Steve Irwin while addressing rumors regarding a host's contract.

topics.title: Howard Stern and Media Rumors
topics.summary: The show discusses media reports regarding Howard Stern's transition to satellite radio, the impact of these rumors on stock prices, and a heated exchange with Bubba the Love Sponge over personal grievances.

topics.title: Racing Competition and News Segment
topics.summary: The studio features a segment on an upcoming racing competition between Bubba and Robin, followed by news reports regarding Oprah Winfrey's potential presidential draft, a legal case involving a teenage offender, Pete Rose’s signed baseballs, and international relations at the United Nations.

transcript.text: Yeah! Alright, good morning everybody, what's happening? I, um... Oh, I don't know. Yeah, I'm trying to get through them, too. It's really good. I poo-pooed at first.

There was a lot of poo. A lot of poo over the years.
You know what I was thinking? You know what I'm kind of envious of? About your life getting into Robin's thing with the $60 million.
Oh, no, you're gonna do it.

Yeah. ...you see her for dinner. You know what I mean? Like, she's grown.
I know. Maybe there's, you know...
Uh, she's grown. Yeah, she's grown. Yeah, but I mean, it must be nice to see her kid.
It is great. It actually is very gratifying, because it's... I think for most... most women...
It doesn't matter, and she seems very gr... nice and great, and you know...
Well, you don't even know what they're talking about. ...she loves you. She loves Artie.
Isn't that funny? Like, she says to me, "How's Artie?" And I go, "Artie's doing really well." I told her about the movie and stuff, and she said, "I love him."
I just love... I said, "Do you know Artie really?" I... I go... She goes...
I thought you'd know her in Las Vegas a little bit when we had dinner that time, and she was...
Maybe this is why I do. She was extra sweet to me. She was very nice and complimentary and stuff.
Yeah, I said to her, "How do you know Artie?" I didn't remember Vegas or, you know, because I got my daughter out to Vegas for her 21st birthday. She joined us.
Yeah, we had that long dinner, and we hung out together. It was fun.
So I didn't remember exactly how she knew you. Right.
Sometimes my kids get mad at me because I don't remember stuff. You know, like, I'm supposed to remember every detail.
They actually get angry. They go, "You don't remember when I met Artie in Vegas?" And I'm like, "Oh, right, right."
Yeah, it's really nice being yelled at. I didn't mark that down on my calendar.
Sometimes my kids can just yell at me for stuff, and I'm like... I mean, I'm barely tracking my life. I don't know how to track yours. I mean, everyone from themselves.
Can't remember yesterday.
I kind of don't remember you meeting Artie now, but...
It wasn't the highlight of your life.
No, it wasn't. I met Artie. I go, "Yeah, I really love Artie." She goes, "How's he doing?" And I go, "You know, I'm talking about Dana."
Was she upset that Artie's away from Dana?
I gave her the whole run. I said, "Artie's up to 282."
Yeah, he's doing great. Well, Dana loved her, too. She met Dana, too. They had a long conversation.
But she just loves you. I mean, like, she goes, "Artie just has a soul. Artie's, like, so considerate."
They must have been on your best behavior that night. Believe me, I was rather.
Yeah, because she goes, "I don't understand the Artie who's got..." You know, I said, "Oh, you mean the gambling, drinking, you know, drug addict, robbing banks."
That's come back.
That might have been after Artie had already done that, and he was being contrite and good.
So she saw good Artie.
I don't know, but, like, I know what she meant. Like, Artie's got a great heart.
Well, remember, it was that trip that Artie almost left early because he had messed up so much, he had to get out of Vegas.
Oh, God. I'm glad my daughter admires her.
That was a trip that I had.
I had to get up the next day and hit the baseball off that broad, and I was just going to get the hell out of here.

Is that who my daughter admires?
Yeah, yeah. Because of the mental issues, I was going through playing that news clipping where I was dead, and, like, "Oh, my God."
Well, anyway, so it was not -- yeah, you're right. It's a cool sort of thing to have, like, this, you know, grown woman as your daughter,
and you can go to dinner and hang out and have a conversation. It's very, very nice, yeah.

And, you know, it was very, very nice. Very nice.
She had a trip this summer. How'd that go?
It went well.
That's good.
So, you know, so we did that, and then I came home, and Beth was out at some movie thing.
And then I quickly jumped into bed.
And started watching Blade?
And started watching Blade. I was in heaven.
So that guy --
She doesn't watch Blade.

Yeah.
The guy that plays the --

reporting that those rumors that Howard Stern
